title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jen Belichick, Bill Belichick's daughter-in-law, is wanted for arrest in North Carolina after missing a court appearance.
Jen received a citation for misdemeanor speeding, allegedly driving 88 mph in a 70 mph zone in Brunswick County.
Bill Belichick is currently head coach at the University of North Carolina, having taken the role in December 2024.

title: Karen Read, Turtleboy look to get witnesses’ defamation suit tossed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read and blogger Aidan Kearney seek to dismiss a defamation lawsuit by witnesses involved in Read's murder case.
Witnesses allege a defamation campaign by Read and Kearney over accusations related to the 2022 death of officer John O’Keefe.
Read and Kearney argue that their actions are protected under the state's anti-SLAPP law to ensure public participation in legal proceedings.

title: The invisible force helping fuel this summer’s intense heat, and all our weather
author: David Epstein
bullets: The jet stream, a high-altitude air current, influences weather patterns, including extreme heat across regions this summer.
Quasi-resonant amplification may explain prolonged heat events, though research on its exact impact is ongoing.
Heat advisories extend from Montana to Maine due to jet stream effects, affecting regions like Boston and Western Europe.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Offensive line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots drafted offensive tackles Will Campbell and Jared Wilson, moving Wilson to center for 2026.
The offensive line allowed 21 postseason sacks, the highest among playoff teams, raising durability concerns.
Patriots' offensive line ranked sixth in pass-block efficiency, aiding QB Drake Maye's MVP-caliber season.

title: New mural brings fresh life to the corner of Doyle’s and the Midway Cafe
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A mural in Jamaica Plain honors Barack Obama and Anthony Bourdain's 2016 visit to Vietnam.
The artwork is part of efforts to revive the area post-Doyle's Cafe's 2019 closure.
Commissioned by Watermark Development, artist Lee Beard completed the mural near new residential and commercial spaces.

title: Largest known bottlenose dolphin stranding hits Cape Cod
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Up to 30 dolphins stranded off Cape Cod at low tide, the largest known mass stranding in the region.
Several dolphins died in Brewster, but remaining ones refloated with the tide, said International Fund for Animal Welfare.
19 live dolphins found across a nine-mile stretch; rescues coordinated to monitor and prevent further strandings.

title: Personal ties are enhancing the tall ships experience at Sail Boston
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Bluenose II from Nova Scotia is a highlight at Sail Boston 2026, reviving historical ties with Boston.
Visitors like Arthur Reed seek out ships with personal connections, enhancing the festival's cultural experience.
Tall ships such as Spirit of Bermuda are offering deck tours and charter sails, enriching Sail Boston 250 festival.

title: ‘I can’t believe that just happened’: Noah Kahan celebrated a hole-in-one at Boston Golf Club
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Musician Noah Kahan hit a hole-in-one on the 11th hole at Boston Golf Club using a five iron.
Kahan shared a video of his hole-in-one on social media, expressing disbelief and excitement.
Kahan recently performed four consecutive sold-out shows at Fenway Park, adding to his successful Boston experience.

title: Boston rents are down again. See how much the average apartment now costs.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Average rent in Boston is now $2,930, down from $3,054 the previous year.
Boston has experienced 13 months of consecutive year-over-year rent declines according to Realtor.com.
Over 37,000 new apartment units in large buildings since 2021 have increased vacancy rates to 3.2%.

title: Mass. teen injured jumping off 64-foot N.H. waterfall
author: John Waller
bullets: A 17-year-old from Massachusetts was injured after jumping off the 64-foot Glen Ellis Falls in New Hampshire.
The teen landed on rocks, resulting in significant but non-life-threatening injuries, according to conservation officers.
He was carried to a parking lot by rescuers and transported to a hospital for treatment.
